title: mentha arvensis subsp. lapponica
intro: Mentha arvensis subsp. lapponica, commonly known as wild mint, is a subspecies of the mint plant that is native to the northern hemisphere, typically found in wetlands and moist areas. The plant possesses medicinal properties due to its high content of menthol, menthone, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of wild mint include its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and provide relief from respiratory problems, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Wild mint can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and topical creams, allowing for a range of applications in traditional medicine and modern herbalism.
